Sentence: CYP2D6 poor metabolizers have reduced conversion of codeine to morphine via O-demethylation.
CYP2D6 ultrarapid metabolizers produce excessive morphine from codeine via enhanced O-demethylation.
CYP2C19 poor metabolizers cannot convert the prodrug clopidogrel to its active thiol metabolite.
CYP2C19 intermediate metabolizers have partially reduced clopidogrel activation.
CYP2D6 poor metabolizers have impaired O-demethylation of tramadol to its active metabolite M1.
CYP3A5 non-expressers (*3/*3) have reduced tacrolimus metabolism leading to higher blood levels.
CYP2C9 poor metabolizers have impaired S-warfarin clearance leading to supra-therapeutic INR.
CYP2C9 intermediate metabolizers have moderately reduced S-warfarin clearance.
CYP2D6 poor metabolizers may have higher sertraline plasma concentrations due to reduced demethylation.
CYP2C19 poor metabolizers show decreased sertraline N-demethylation.
